title: KO looks for credentials in ~/.docker/config.json,  podman on Mac OS does not create the file
body:
I am building the OpenShift Pipelines Operator, ( https://github.com/tektoncd/operator), from my MacBook.

I removed Docker Desktop after they changed the licensing.  My job and my hobby blur the lines between personal and professional use, so I did not want to take any legal risk with Docker.

As an alternative, I am using Podman:

```bash
podman machine init
podman machine start
```

podman version 3.3.1

When building the Tekton Operator:

```bash
export KO_DOCKER_REPO=${LOCAL_REGISTRY}/tekton
make TARGET=openshift CR=config/default apply 
```

I get the following error:

```bash
2021/10/11 13:39:28 Publishing nexus.clg.lab:5001/tekton/operator-1d69a75f22dd094880847eac907fb2c1:latest
2021/10/11 13:39:28 Publishing nexus.clg.lab:5001/tekton/webhook-340ad78e88ca5477447aa144fedfe1a1:latest
error: no objects passed to apply
Error: error processing import paths in "-": error resolving image references: POST https://nexus.clg.lab:5001/v2/tekton/operator-1d69a75f22dd094880847eac907fb2c1/blobs/uploads/: UNAUTHORIZED: access to the requested resource is not authorized
2021/10/11 13:39:28 error during command execution: error processing import paths in "-": error resolving image references: POST https://nexus.clg.lab:5001/v2/tekton/operator-1d69a75f22dd094880847eac907fb2c1/blobs/uploads/: UNAUTHORIZED: access to the requested resource is not authorized
```

After copying `~/.config/containers/auth.json` to `~/.docker/config.json` everything works.

If this is isolated to `KO`, then maybe the issue is there, but other apps may also default to looking for credentials in `~/.docker/config.json`

Any advice?

Thanks

comments.body: We could create a link between the two files. Not sure the best way to handle this.  We could talk to techtoncd about this.

Bubbling up my comment in https://github.com/tektoncd/operator/issues/448#issuecomment-942326972

> [Looking at the code](https://github.com/google/go-containerregistry/blob/72ae53c6cd6746b48ee64f08aa8b504d4d975a00/pkg/authn/keychain.go#L65), you should be able to workaround this in the meantime by setting `DOCKER_CONFIG=~/.config/containers/auth.json`.

@imjasonh Thanks!

I'll add that to the tutorial in my blog post.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I don't see this as something we can fix.
